title: Gulf states harden positions against Iran as economic toll of war mounts
description:
As Trump negotiates, Arab states seem to be pushing for war to continue until certain demands are met, driven by the damage wreaked by Hormuz's closure and attacks on energy sites

title: Oil prices rise as Iran war escalates with Houthi attacks
description: Oil prices climbed above $116 per barrel after markets opened Sunday, as Iran-backed Houthi militants fired missiles and drones at Israel over the weekend and promised to continue attacks.Why it matters: The high price that remained around the $116 a barrel mark late Sunday came as the war moved into its fifth week, showing little sign of abating despite President Trump's talk of diplomacy. title: Iran and proxies continue attacking energy facilities, residential areas, and Arab capitals (March 24–27 updates)
description:
Between March 24 and March 27, Iran and its allied militias continued launching drones and missiles against Arab countries. The attacks targeted energy infrastructure, airports, military facilities, ports, and residential areas in Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, Bahrain, Iraq, and Jordan.

title: Bab al-Mandab strait: Iran’s Houthi allies enter the conflict, raising fears over key trade route
description: As Iran’s Houthi allies enter the conflict, Iran has threatened to disrupt shipping in the Bab al-Mandab Strait – a strategic waterway linking the Gulf of Aden to the Red Sea and the Suez Canal – with the help of its Houthi allies in Yemen. While disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz primarily affect Asia, blocking access to the Gulf of Aden could affect European energy supplies and imports from Asia.

title: Yemen’s Houthis enter Iran war with drone and missile attacks on Israel

The Houthis launched missile and drone attacks against Israel, the first direct military action since the beginning of the US and Israeli campaign against Iran that began on February 28. title: Russia reportedly sending Iran drones for use against the US and Israel
description:
Reports citing Western officials suggest that Russia has started supplying Iran with complete drones after Russia was previously suspected of only providing components to improve Iranian systems. The unmanned aerial systems are likely modified, Russian-made versions of the same Shahed-136 one-way attack drones that Tehran previously supplied to Moscow.

description:
The US and Israel continued airstrikes as negotiations over the passage of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz hit an impasse and Iran ramped up efforts to repress its populace. The regime has expanded internal security checkpoints into a dispersed network across various cities and increased arrests targeting alleged collaborators and dissenters.

title: New Paris mayor vows to tackle systemic abuse in after-school programmes
description: Paris' new mayor Emmanuel Grégoire officially started on March 30. His first promise has been to tackle child abuse in after-school programmes, an issue that was at the centre of the latest municipal elections. Emmanuel Grégoire was himself a victim of abuse in an after-school programme, which he made public last September. The issue is not specific to Paris - cases have appeared all over France. FRANCE 24's Solange Mougin looks into the issue.

title: Zelensky hails 'historic' defence agreements with Gulf states
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Monday said he signed "historic" agreements with countries in the Middle East last week, including sharing Ukrainian expertise on downing drones as Iranian strikes continue to target Gulf countries. Ukraine has previously proposed an exchange of its drone interceptors for the Gulf's air defence missiles.

description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Michael Knights, Adjunct Fellow at The Washington Institute and Head of Research at Horizon Engage. He examines the evolving military dynamics between the US and Iran, focusing on the operational possibilities available to US forces and the structural resilience of Iran’s defence strategy. According to Knights, decisive, large-scale invasions are highly unlikely. He argues that while the US possesses the capacit

title: While Trump vows Iran deal on the way, 'evidence points to another direction'
description: US President Donald Trump threatened on March 30 to destroy Iran's crude export hub of Kharg Island, along with its oil wells and power plants, unless Tehran quickly accepted a peace deal. He also claimed that the United States was in 'serious discussions' with 'a more reasonable regime' in Tehran. 'While Trump points in one direction, empirical evidence seems to point the other way' says FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert.

title: South Lebanon: Locals remain as war rages on
description:
🇱🇧 "Nowhere is safe."
 
 
 
Mere kilometres from the #Israel-#Lebanon border, FRANCE 24 accompanied a humanitarian aid convoy headed to the town of #Marjayoun.
 
 
 
For the thousands that still remain in the region, convoys like this are their last resort for essential necessities.
 
 
 
Our reporters Melina Huet and Achraf Abid bring you this story.

title: France hands captain of suspected Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker one-year jail sentence
description: A French court on Monday sentenced in absentia the captain of a suspected Russian shadow fleet tanker to one year in jail for failing to comply with orders to stop his ship. The vessel, which the French navy boarded in September before releasing the vessel, was thought to be transporting Russian oil in violation of Western sanctions.

title: Russian oil arrives in Cuba following Trump turnaround on Cuban blockade
description: A Russian oil tanker has reached Cuba, bringing a much-needed shipment to the oil-starved nation. The shipment of oil would be the first to Havana since January, when US forces abducted Venezuelan president and Cuban ally Nicolas Maduro in a raid. This comes after US President Donald Trump signalled to a softening of the blockade against the island nation. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle looks into this change of course from Trump, and what could be beh

title: Generation Jihad | The Kharg Island Trap
description:
Kharg Island looks like the perfect target — take it, and you choke off Iran’s oil. But it’s not that simple. Bill Roggio is joined by Ryan Brobst and Cameron McMillan to discuss why seizing Kharg could hand Tehran exactly what it wants: a wider war, a vulnerable US position, and a fight on the regime’s terms.
The post Generation Jihad | The Kharg Island Trap appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: Israel continues operations against Hamas in Gaza, diplomats push for disarmament as shaky ceasefire continues
description:
While attention has shifted to the conflicts in Iran and Lebanon, Gaza remains under a fragile ceasefire, with ongoing low-level clashes. High Representative for Gaza Nickolay Mladenov highlighted progress toward a disarmament framework tied to UN-backed plans. Despite this, Hamas continues to consolidate control in parts of the territory as Israel targets militants and terrorist infrastructure.
The post Israel continues operations against Hamas

title: Turkish intelligence chief hosts Hamas leaders in Istanbul
description:
Turkish spy chief Ibrahim Kalin’s March 22 meeting with senior Hamas leaders in Istanbul highlights Turkey’s continued high-level engagement with the group, reinforcing Ankara’s role as a mediator in Gaza ceasefire negotiations while omitting any discussion of Hamas’s disarmament. The meeting reflects a broader pattern of Turkish support for the group, evidenced by recent US Treasury sanctions on Turkish charities linked to Hamas.

title: Asian economies scramble to find solutions to war's impact on oil markets
description: Iran continues to cut off the strait of Hormuz as it retaliates for the US and Israel's campaign against the Islamic Republic. The strategy has been effective as it piles pressure on the United States to the end the war, with global energy prices continuing to soar. Over 80% of oil and gas typically passing through the Strait of Hormuz heads for Asia.

title: One Lebanese soldier killed, Israel expands buffer zone in southern Lebanon
description: Israel renewed its bombardment of Beirut's southern suburbs on March 30 while continuing air strikes on Lebanon's south, one of which targeted an army checkpoint and killed a soldier. title: Asian economies most vulnerable to impact of Middle east war on oil markets
description: More than 80% of the crude oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG) that passes through Strait of Hormuz heads to Asia. With the war in the Middle East giving no sign of slowing, many parts of Southeast Asia have suffered fuel supply difficulties. Now, policymakers in the Asia-Pacific region are facing their toughest test since the COVID-19 pandemic. FRANCE 24's Rochelle Ferguson Bouyahi takes a closer look.

title: US military building up in the Middle East as Trump looks for offramp in Iran
description: President Donald Trump said he wanted to take Iran’s oil and suggested US troops could seize the country’s Kharg Island export hub, even as he added in comments published on March 30 that a deal with Tehran to end the war “could be made fairly quickly.” The buildup has since been confirmed by the New York Times, who says hundreds of commandos have arrived in the Middle East. title: Singer-songwriter China Moses on why her music is not on streaming platforms
description: A Franco-American jazz and soul singer has spoken to FRANCE 24 about her love for music and her deep reservations of streaming platforms like Spotify. China Moses is the daughter of jazz singer Dee Dee Bridgewater and director Gilbert Moses. Her latest album is called "It's complicated". She spoke to us in Perspective about how her music is published, staying off most streaming devices.

Washington is mulling a ground offensive to seize Iran's nuclear stockpile, which would be one of the riskiest in US history. Thousands of American army personnel arriving in the region over the weekend, as Trump also signalled to a possible seizure of Kharg island.

title: ‘We’re killing them, bro’: Israelis correct Iranian online streamer
description: Iranian musician thanks young Israeli women for ‘saving’ Iran through US-Israeli air strikes.

title: Trump says Iran’s ‘had regime change’ as he describes ‘boatloads of oil’
description: US President Donald Trump says the US-Israeli war on Iran has effectively brought ‘regime change’.

title: In Australia, Victoria and Tasmania Waive Transit Fares as Fuel Prices Soar
description: The measures in Victoria and Tasmania are temporary, but show how far and how intensely the oil shock from the war in Iran has spread.

title: Asia Depends on L.N.G. From the Middle East. This Is What Happens When It Runs Out.
description: With a cutoff in shipments imminent, Asian countries, the biggest importers of liquefied natural gas from the Middle East, are already burning more coal and reducing consumption.

title: The Slum in Gangnam, the Richest Part of Seoul
description: The city wants to redevelop a shantytown in Gangnam district, where hundreds are defying eviction, fighting for a right to own a home in an area notorious for the exorbitant cost of housing.

title: Trump Says Iran Agreed to Allow 20 More Ships of Oil Through Strait of Hormuz
description: The president characterized Iran’s permission as a “sign of respect.” Israel said it launched a wave of strikes targeting infrastructure in Tehran and other areas, and Iran retaliated.

title: США перебросили на Ближний Восток отряды сил специальных операций
description: Пентагон перебросил на Ближний Восток несколько сотен военнослужащих сил специальных операций, сообщила газета The New York Times со ссылкой на двух американских военных представителей.

title: WP: эксперты сомневаются в возможности США уничтожить ракетную программу Ирана
description: Эксперты сомневаются в возможности США и Израиля полностью уничтожить ракетную программу Ирана, пишет The Washington Post. Читать далее

title: Air China возобновила авиасообщение между Пекином и Пхеньяном
description: Китайская авиакомпания Air China возобновила прямое пассажирское авиасообщение между столицами Китая и КНДР, сообщило агентство «Синьхуа». Reuters со ссылкой на соучредителя туроператора Young Pioneer Tours сообщает, что авиарейсы между Пекином и Пхеньяном с 30 марта будут еженедельными.

title: Police, Catholic patriarchate reach agreement on prayer at Holy Sepulchre after cardinal blocked
description:
Netanyahu orders 'full and immediate access' for Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa after he and another Catholic leader are barred entry to holy site due to wartime restrictions 
The post Police, Catholic patriarchate reach agreement on prayer at Holy Sepulchre after cardinal blocked appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Blackouts hit parts of Tehran as Israel conducts strikes in Iran amid peace talks
description:
Electricity back in some areas, power grid stable, state media reports; Pakistan says it will be 'honored' to host US-Iran talks soon, but unclear if either side agreed to attend; Iran floats proposal for end of war
The post Blackouts hit parts of Tehran as Israel conducts strikes in Iran amid peace talks appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Trump: Killing Iran’s leaders is ‘regime change,’ deal to end war ‘could be soon’
description:
US president also says Iran agreed to let 20 more boats through Strait of Hormuz, and that he'd like to 'take the oil in Iran' * UNIFIL says one peacekeeper killed, one critically injured in south Lebanon explosion
The post Trump: Killing Irans leaders is regime change, deal to end war could be soon appeared first on The Times of Israel.
